response.justification: The candidate lacks the required 5–10 years of professional C++ development experience on Windows, with no demonstrated work in C++ since 2015. While they have strong polyglot programming experience and financial domain exposure (investment practices at RIDE Capital), the absence of modern C++ experience, SQL Server/Oracle proficiency, and fixed-income domain knowledge make them a poor fit for this role.
strengths: Strong polyglot programming background with experience in multiple languages and frameworks
Demonstrated financial domain exposure through work at RIDE Capital (investment practices)
Proven ability to learn and adapt to new technologies quickly
concerns: No recent or substantial C++ development experience (last C++ work was in 2015 at National Instruments)
No direct experience with SQL Server or Oracle, nor Windows platform C++ development
Lacks fixed-income or fixed-income derivatives domain expertise, which is mandatory for this role

key_factors.factor: Fixed income or capital markets domain expertise
